---
title: "SEM-cowpea"
author: "Durga Chinthalapudi"
date: "2026-02-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(lavaan)
library(semPlot)
library(semTools)
library(performance)
library(lavaanPlot)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
```


```{r}
dat = read.csv("Sujan-physiology.csv")
```


```{r}
# ---- Recode treatments / factors ----
meta <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    SampleID = as.character(SampleID),
    Genotype = str_trim(as.character(Genotype)) %>% factor(),
    Stage    = str_trim(as.character(Stage)),
    Treatment = str_trim(as.character(Treatment))
  )
```

```{r}
meta <- meta %>%
  mutate(
    Treatment = str_trim(as.character(Treatment)),
    Treatment_bin = case_when(
      str_to_lower(Treatment) %in% c("control","c") ~ 0,
      str_to_lower(Treatment) %in% c("drought","d","stress") ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

meta <- meta %>%
  mutate(
    Stage = str_trim(as.character(Stage)),
    # set correct order for your dataset
    Stage = factor(Stage, levels = c("V2-Stage","V4-Stage","R1-Stage","R4-Stage"), ordered = TRUE),
    Stage_num = as.numeric(Stage)
  )

```


```{r}
vars_cont <- c(
  "pH","NAG","BG",
  "AWCD","Polymers","Carbohydrates","Carboxylic_acids","Amino_acids","Amines","Phenolic_acids",
  "gsw","E","ChlM","NFI","Plant_Height","Node_no",
  "composi_pcoa","composi_nmds",
  "diversity_shan"   # your RESPONSE
)
```



```{r}
sem_dat <- meta %>%
  select(SampleID, Genotype, Stage, Stage_num, Treatment_bin, Replication, all_of(vars_cont)) %>%
  drop_na()


```

```{r}
sem_dat[vars_cont] <- scale(sem_dat[vars_cont])
```

```{r}
check_collinearity(
  lm(diversity_shan ~ pH + NAG + BG + AWCD + gsw + ChlM + composi_pcoa,
     data = sem_dat)
)
```


```{r}
model_v2 <- paste(
  "# Drivers of soil + plant",
  "pH   ~ a1*Treatment_bin + a2*Stage_num + a3*Genotype",
  "gsw  ~ g1*Treatment_bin + g2*Stage_num + g3*Genotype",
  "ChlM ~ h1*Treatment_bin + h2*Stage_num + h3*Genotype",
  "",
  "# Composition is structured by experimental design (core!)",
  "composi_pcoa ~ t1*Treatment_bin + t2*Stage_num + t3*Genotype +",
  "              p1*pH + p2*gsw + p3*ChlM",
  "",
  "# Diversity",
  "diversity_shan ~ y1*Treatment_bin + y2*Stage_num + y3*Genotype +",
  "                 y4*pH + y5*gsw + y6*ChlM + y7*composi_pcoa",
  "",
  "# Residual correlations (minimal)",
  "gsw ~~ ChlM",
  "",
  "# Indirect effects via composition",
  "ind_T := t1*y7",
  "ind_G := t3*y7",
  "ind_S := t2*y7",
  "tot_T := y1 + ind_T",
  "tot_G := y3 + ind_G",
  "tot_S := y2 + ind_S",
  sep = "\n"
)

fit_v2 <- lavaan::sem(
  model = model_v2,
  data = sem_dat,
  estimator = "MLR",
  std.lv = TRUE,
  missing = "fiml"
)


summary(fit_v2, standardized = TRUE, fit.measures = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Standardized path table
paths_std <- standardizedSolution(fit_v2) %>%
  dplyr::filter(op == "~") %>%
  dplyr::select(lhs, rhs, est.std, se, z, pvalue) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(est.std)))

paths_std

```

```{r}

lavaanPlot(model = fit_v2, stand = TRUE, coefs = TRUE,
           stars = "regress", sig = 0.05, covs = FALSE,
           node_options = list(shape = "rectangle"),
           edge_options = list(fontsize = 10),
           graph_options = list(rankdir = "TB"))
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
